SEC-1.title: Global, unconditional deny list
SEC-1.statement: The deny list is global and unconditional, evaluated before tier gating, in every mode.

regulatory.note: risk management: globally denied operations never execute

SEC-2.title: Single audited execution path with minted approvals
SEC-2.statement: Exactly one ordered pipeline; every call (success, failure, denial) writes an audit record; approval is a server-minted, single-use, TTL-bound nonce surfaced out-of-band.

regulatory.note: human oversight via the approval gate

SEC-3.title: Conservative classification (round up)
SEC-3.statement: Classification rounds up on ambiguity; sudo/doas/pkexec are at least T2.

SEC-4.title: Lethal-trifecta containment
SEC-4.statement: A session never holds sensitive data plus untrusted content plus actuation without a human gate; read tools are separable from act tools; collected data is untrusted.

regulatory.note: data protection by design: read/act separation

SEC-5.title: host_type gates actuation
SEC-5.statement: Actuation branches on host_type; SSH is refused for a Talos host; SSH carries a host-key policy and an option-injection-safe target; talosctl is verb-allowlisted and node-aware at T3.

regulatory.note: asset and configuration management: actuation respects the host paradigm

SEC-6.title: Human-gated convergence
SEC-6.statement: Convergence is DRY_RUN then approve then execute; no finding auto-fixes; the target and baseline currency are validated.

SEC-7.title: Transport posture (stdio default; HTTP fail-closed)
SEC-7.statement: stdio by default; any non-loopback bind requires token AND explicit opt-in AND an SSRF egress filter; no token passthrough.

regulatory.note: secure by default: stdio default, fail-closed HTTP

SEC-8.title: Scoped credentials, kill switch, contained logger
SEC-8.statement: Credentials are scoped, revocable, never logged; the kill switch stops execution immediately with an operator actuator and a durable sentinel; logger construction never raises; an actuation subprocess is isolated.

regulatory.note: risk-management measures: least privilege, kill switch

SEC-9.title: Audit stores hash and length, never bodies; params redacted
SEC-9.statement: The audit log stores output_sha256 and output_len only, never bodies; parameters are redacted; output is truncated; the log file is owner-only.

regulatory.note: data protection by design: no bodies stored
security of processing: redaction of secrets

SEC-10.title: Append-only bitemporal state
SEC-10.statement: State facts are append-only; deletion is blocked at the storage layer; supersession carries actor and reason; one active fact per (subject, predicate, fact_type).

regulatory.note: accuracy and robustness: a durable source of truth

CTL-001.title: Supply-chain integrity and secure development
CTL-001.statement: Digest-pinned image, SBOM, dependency review, SHA-pinned CI, CodeQL, and nightly fuzz; mypy strict and ruff (bandit S-rules) enforce secure coding. Tracked residuals: real image digest and CycloneDX/zarf parity.

regulatory.note: no known exploitable vulnerabilities; supply-chain security
vulnerability handling: CodeQL, nightly fuzz, STPA revisit triggers
secure coding: mypy strict, ruff, CodeQL, fuzz
